chunks.name: Sparse Episodic Memory for Missions

Router registered, endpoints live at /api/episodes/retrieve and /api/missions/{id}/episodes — VERIFIED via OpenAPI + 401 (auth required) on unauth curl
Worker is event-driven (subscribes to substrate mission.completed events) — VERIFIED by code inspection: no LLM call in _build_summary

bugfix_by_orchestrator.bug: Migration `20260612_episodic_memory_001.py` used a multi-statement `op.execute()` for the trigger function + trigger creation. asyncpg prepared statements cannot handle multi-statement SQL — the deploy script reported 'Migrations applied successfully' but the head was still at `auth_v3_feature_flag_001` (the new migration silently failed). The deploy script does not fail the build on migration errors.
bugfix_by_orchestrator.fix: Split the single `op.execute("""...""")` into two separate `op.execute()` calls (one for the function, one for the trigger). Applied via `docker cp` into the running container, then `alembic upgrade head` succeeded. Trigger fire-tested in postgres: `tsvector_populated = t`.
bugfix_by_orchestrator.root_cause: asyncpg (the async PG driver) prepares each statement individually and cannot batch multiple statements into a single prepare. SQLAlchemy's `op.execute()` on a multi-statement string goes through asyncpg's prepared-statement path which rejects the multi-statement input.
bugfix_by_orchestrator.deployed_via: `docker cp` + `alembic upgrade head` in container. Full backend rebuild deferred to next normal deploy (the fixed file is committed to git, so next `deploy-backend.sh` will bake it into the image).

bugs.bug: POST endpoint `/api/tool-routing/route` was registered but SKIPPED by FastAPI's OpenAPI generator with `WARNING OpenAPI: 1 routes skipped due to unresolved forward refs: /api/tool-routing/route`. Root cause: `from __future__ import annotations` in `tool_routing_models.py` made all type hints strings (PEP 563), and Pydantic v2 could not resolve `list[ToolScore]` inside `ToolRouteResult` without an explicit `model_rebuild()` call. The endpoint may also fail at request time when Pydantic validates the response against the unresolved schema.
bugs.fix: Removed `from __future__ import annotations` from `tool_routing_models.py`. With the import removed, type hints are evaluated at class definition time, and `ToolScore` is already defined when `ToolRouteResult` references it. Verified by re-instantiating the model and re-running all 19 unit tests.
bugs.root_cause: PEP 563 + Pydantic v2: with `from __future__ import annotations`, all annotations are stored as strings. Pydantic v2 needs `model_rebuild()` to resolve forward refs in the same module. The `list[ToolScore]` reference inside `ToolRouteResult` qualifies as a forward ref under PEP 563 even though `ToolScore` is defined first in the file.
bugs.deployed_via: Local edit only. Container restart + `bash /opt/flowmanner/deploy-backend.sh` required to pick up the fix in the running backend (Docker images have no volume mounts, per AGENTS.md).

bugs.bug: `alembic upgrade head` did NOT apply the new `tool_routing_001` migration during the deploy. The deploy script said 'Migrations applied successfully' but `alembic current` was still at `episodic_memory_001` after the deploy. The migration code IS valid (single-statement `op.execute()` for the partial index) — verified by manual `alembic upgrade head` working perfectly.
bugs.fix: Orchestrator manually ran `docker compose exec -T backend alembic upgrade head` to apply the migration. Head is now at `tool_routing_001 (head)`. The `tool_routing_decisions` table exists with all 3 indexes (composite, partial mission, pkey).
bugs.root_cause: `deploy-backend.sh` line 213-214 always prints 'Migrations applied successfully' via `log_success` regardless of whether `alembic upgrade head` actually moved the head. The script does not capture stderr or check the exit code. This is the same deploy-script bug as Chunk 2 (caught there with the asyncpg multi-statement migration).
bugs.deploy_script_fix_recommended: FIXED in commit 86c76fa (2026-06-13). The deploy script now (1) runs migrations AFTER build so the container has the latest migration files baked in, (2) verifies `alembic current` matches `alembic heads` after migration, and (3) auto-rollbacks on migration failure. See .sisyphus/evidence/deploy-script-migration-verification-2026-06-13.txt for full details.

bugs.bug: GET /missions/{id}/depth-events endpoint was on a router with prefix='/depth', making the actual mount /api/depth/missions/{mission_id}/depth-events — NOT the documented /api/missions/{mission_id}/depth-events.  Sub-agent's self-report and the file docstring both stated the correct (documented) path, but neither matched the actual FastAPI route table.  Any client trying to use the endpoint at the documented path would have gotten 404.
bugs.fix: Split the router in backend/app/api/v1/depth.py: kept `router = APIRouter(prefix='/depth', tags=['depth'])` for the POST /decide admin endpoint, added a separate `events_router = APIRouter(tags=['depth-events'])` for the GET /missions/{id}/depth-events mission-scoped replay endpoint.  Registered `depth_events_router` in backend/app/api/v1/__init__.py:128 and added it to the for-loop at line 239 with name 'depth-events'.  Endpoint now mounts at /api/missions/{mission_id}/depth-events as documented.
bugs.verification: Added 3 regression tests in backend/tests/test_depth_routing.py that import the FastAPI app and assert the correct paths exist and the buggy path does NOT.  All 3 pass.
bugs.root_cause: The depth.py file was generated by the sub-agent with a single APIRouter(prefix='/depth', ...) and a /missions/{id}/depth-events path inside it.  FastAPI concatenates the prefix and the path, so the actual mount included /depth/.  No review of the actual route table was done before claiming chunk completion.

bugs.bug: The reason field on DepthDecision concatenated ALL 4 signal reasons with '; ' separator.  For a high-risk DEEP decision, the reason string included 'uncertainty=0.20 < 0.3', 'prior_failures=0', and 'budget=$5.00 (adequate)' as if those non-binding signals mattered.  This made audit/replay logs misleading.
bugs.fix: Refactored _combine_decisions in depth_policy.py to return the winning _CandidateDecision (not just its level).  decide() now uses the winner's reason verbatim and only appends '; HITL: <reason>' when HITL escalates.  The full per-signal values are still in the audit event payload (DepthTriggeredEvent has separate fields for risk, uncertainty, budget_remaining_usd, prior_failures, etc.) so replay completeness is preserved.
bugs.verification: Added 4 regression tests in test_depth_policy.py::TestReasonFormat asserting the reason is winner-only (no semicolons unless HITL appended).  All 4 pass; the 21 original tests still pass.

bugs.bug: _emit_depth_event opened its own AsyncSessionLocal() to write the DEPTH_DECIDED audit event, committing in a SEPARATE transaction from the parent mission execution.  If the parent mission transaction rolled back, the depth event would STAY in the DB, causing audit/replay divergence.
bugs.fix: Removed the inner 'async with AsyncSessionLocal() as event_db' block; the function now uses the caller's `db` parameter directly when calling event_log.append().  This keeps the event atomically consistent with the parent transaction (rolls back together).  Updated docstring to document the change and the reasoning.  The try/except wrapper still makes the audit non-critical (event failure does not abort mission execution).
bugs.verification: No regression test added (would require full integration test with DB rollback).  Verified by code review.

HandoffPacket schema has 7 required fields (goal, success_criteria, retrieved_context_ids, tool_candidates, budget_remaining_usd, hitl_state, depth_policy_state)
HANDOFF_INITIATED = 'handoff.initiated'
HANDOFF_ACCEPTED = 'handoff.accepted'
HANDOFF_COMPLETED = 'handoff.completed'
HANDOFF_FAILED = 'handoff.failed'
HANDOFF_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED = 'handoff.budget_exhausted'
HANDOFF_LEASE_LOST = 'handoff.lease_lost' (emitted from accept_with_packet when renew returns False)
delegate_with_packet emits HANDOFF_INITIATED via event_log.append
complete_with_packet with spent_usd > remaining_usd raises BudgetExceededError AND emits HANDOFF_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED (NOT HANDOFF_COMPLETED)
Cross-workspace HITL item in packet raises ValueError (no cross-tenant leakage)
HandoffLeaseIntegration wraps LeaseManager primitives (claim/renew/release/transfer)
No breaking changes to HandoffRecord schema (all new columns nullable)

23/23 unit tests pass (21 original + 2 orchestrator regression tests: HANDOFF_LEASE_LOST emission, _packet_from_record old-record fallback) — VERIFIED: `23 passed in 0.18s`
2/2 new HandoffLeaseIntegration unit tests pass (transfer with/without new_worker_id) — VERIFIED: `2 passed in 0.04s`
3 integration tests properly marked @pytest.mark.integration (skipped in dev env, expected) — VERIFIED

bugs.bug: HANDOFF_LEASE_LOST event type was added to the SubstrateEventType enum (handoff.lease_lost) but NEVER emitted from any code path.  Defined in substrate_models.py:132, zero emission sites.  The whole point of the event was to capture worker-churn / lost-lease cases for substrate audit, but the implementation never wired it up.  Without it, an operator looking at the substrate event log has no visibility into 'why did this handoff's accept happen 30s after the previous worker stopped'.
bugs.fix: In handoff_protocol.py accept_with_packet(), capture the return of self.lease_integration.renew(handoff_id).  If False, emit HANDOFF_LEASE_LOST via _emit_handoff_event with payload {handoff_id, phase='accept', reason='lease renewal returned False', to_agent_id}, and log a warning.  Fail-open preserved — the handoff is still accepted (matches pre-fix behavior) so existing callers don't break, but the audit event is now in the substrate log.
bugs.root_cause: The sub-agent's commit message said 'HANDOFF_LEASE_LOST' was added but the implementation stopped at defining the enum value.  No test exercised the 'lease lost' path; no call site was wired up.  This is a chunk-5 'spec half-implemented' bug — the event type existed, the code path that should have emitted it was never written.
bugs.verification: New unit test test_accept_with_packet_emits_lease_lost_when_renew_fails — asserts that when renew() returns False, both 'handoff.lease_lost' and 'handoff.accepted' events are emitted.  PASSES.

bugs.bug: HandoffLeaseIntegration.transfer(handoff_id, from_agent_id, to_agent_id) took from_agent_id and to_agent_id as required parameters but NEVER USED them.  The function called self.release(handoff_id) (which uses self._worker_id) and then self.claim_for_handoff(handoff_id, to_agent_id) (which also uses self._worker_id).  Result: no worker_id change at all — the function refreshes the lease under the SAME worker.  The docstring claimed 'Transfer lease ownership during worker churn' and the spec said worker churn is the primary use case, but the implementation did not perform a cross-worker transfer.  The existing integration test test_lease_transfer 'passed' but only checked that lease is not None — it never verified the worker_id actually changed (because it didn't).
bugs.fix: Refactored lease_integration.py: extracted _claim_for_worker(handoff_id, agent_id, worker_id) as the internal claim primitive that takes an explicit worker_id.  claim_for_handoff() and transfer() now both call it.  transfer() gained a new optional parameter new_worker_id: str | None = None — if provided, the re-claim happens under that worker; if None, the instance's self._worker_id is used (preserves pre-fix behavior, no breaking change).  Default-None keeps existing callers working; new callers that need real cross-worker transfer pass the new worker_id explicitly.
bugs.root_cause: The class wraps a single worker_id per instance, so a single HandoffLeaseIntegration can only claim under one worker.  The transfer method needed to accept a different worker_id for the re-claim but the original signature only took from_agent_id/to_agent_id (agent IDs, not worker IDs).  The from_agent_id and to_agent_id params were vestigial — they were meant for logging/audit but never wired into the actual claim call.
bugs.verification: Two new unit tests in test_handoff_lease_integration.py::TestHandoffLeaseTransferUnit: test_transfer_without_new_worker_id_uses_instance_default (verifies backward compat) and test_transfer_with_new_worker_id_uses_new_worker (verifies the new behavior).  Both PASS.

bugs.bug: _packet_from_record() in handoff_protocol.py called HandoffBudget(remaining_usd=Decimal('0'), initial_usd=Decimal('0')) for pre-chunk-5 records where handoff.budget_remaining_usd is NULL.  But HandoffBudget's model field initial_usd has constraint gt=0 (must be greater than zero), so this raised pydantic_core._pydantic_core.ValidationError: Input should be greater than 0.  In practice: any call to accept_with_packet on a pre-chunk-5 record (e.g., an existing handoff that was created before chunk 5 deployed) would crash with a 500 error.  The 49dc90f followup fix added a success_criteria fallback but missed the budget fallback.
bugs.fix: In _packet_from_record, when handoff.budget_remaining_usd is None, set remaining=Decimal('0') and initial=Decimal('0.000001') (the smallest valid positive Decimal that satisfies gt=0).  Updated the docstring to explain the fallback: 'For pre-chunk-5 records where budget_remaining_usd is NULL, we cannot recover either remaining or initial from the row, so we use Decimal('0') for remaining (matches 'no budget recorded') and Decimal('0.000001') for initial (the smallest valid positive Decimal, satisfying HandoffBudget's gt=0).'  Callers that need the true original initial should read the HANDOFF_INITIATED event payload.
bugs.root_cause: The 49dc90f followup was a 'small fix' for two related issues (from_agent_name required + success_criteria fallback).  The author tested with mock records that had non-NULL budget_remaining_usd, so the NULL case was never exercised.  The test test_packet_from_record_old_record_fallback catches this gap.
bugs.verification: New unit test test_packet_from_record_old_record_fallback constructs a mock HandoffRecord with goal=None, success_criteria=None, budget_remaining_usd=None, etc. and asserts that _packet_from_record returns a valid HandoffPacket.  PASSES.

chunks.name: Substrate Replay API Hardening & Cross-Tenant Safety
stop_gates_planned: Cross-tenant safety on every substrate.py endpoint (3+ tests, 404 not 403)
Filter by event_type (single, CSV, unknown)
Deterministic ordering with (timestamp, sequence) tiebreak — byte-identical replay
Pagination with after_sequence + limit, defaults 100, max 1000
All chunk 2-6 event types are replayable (12+ event-type tests)
Backward-compat: existing endpoint shapes preserved
Substrate baseline preserved (145 pass / 3 pre-existing failures, no new failures)
git diff --check clean
make validate-migration passes (or fails on known community_templates drift per issue #1)
alembic current == alembic heads after any migration (default: no migration needed)
No docker cp, no try/except pass, no PEP 563 in new Pydantic models
chunks.notes: Hardening chunk, not feature chunk. No new event types, no LLM code, no strategy changes. Closes the cross-tenant weakness in _require_owner (chunk 5 HITL scoping pattern). Consolidates the per-event-type routers from chunks 3/4/5/6 behind a safe unified API; consolidation of those routers is deferred (backward-compat).

canonical_baseline_summary: 151 pass, 10 pre-existing failures (5 PG-DNS env-only, 5 mission_executor test-rot/interface-mismatch)
runner_notes: User runs DeepSeek via codebuff CLI; orchestrator writes prompt file at .hermes/plans/, DeepSeek reads it. Orchestrator verifies result and updates state. Orchestrator also catches sub-agent bugs (e.g. asyncpg multi-statement migration bug in Chunk 2) and applies targeted fixes.
git_workflow_notes: No PR per chunk. Sub-agent commits and pushes directly. User reviews via session audit, not via GitHub PRs. Frontend commits go to the frontend repo's master branch; backend commits go to this repo's main.
